brief: The highest-yield opportunities are concentrated in Brooklyn market-tier parcels utilizing 485-x tax exemptions combined with Section 8 VPS rents, generating cash-on-cash returns between 12.0% and 31.8%. Out of 1,271 scanned lots, 918 pencil, with 289 city-owned sites showing significant land cost headroom above estimated comps. Bronx program plays offer up to $333,039 in developer fees under an ELLA / 4% LIHTC stack but require competitive subsidy awards and near-zero land acquisition costs.

targets.play: Develop a 10-unit building in an R7-2 zone utilizing the 485-x tax exemption and Section 8 VPS rents to target a 31.8% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $569,141 versus a walk-away land cap of $3,202,693 provides $2,633,552 in valuation headroom.
targets.risk: Property is city-owned, requiring navigating the public HPD/EDC disposition process rather than a private acquisition, along with R7-2 zoning verification.

targets.play: Construct a 14-unit residential project in an R7A zone leveraging 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents for an 18.3% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $690,612 versus a walk-away land ceiling of $2,337,060 leaves $1,646,448 in pricing headroom.
targets.risk: City-owned status requires an HPD disposition RFP, alongside site condition and environmental verification.

targets.play: Build 12 units in an R7A zone executing the 485-x plus Section 8 VPS rent strategy for a 16.9% cash-on-cash yield.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $591,738 compared to a $1,850,943 walk-away land cap gives $1,259,205 in headroom.
targets.risk: Subject to HPD disposition approval timelines and detailed site physical condition diligence.

targets.play: Execute a 10-unit all-affordable program build via the ELLA / 4% LIHTC stack to earn a $333,039 developer fee.
targets.negotiation: Estimated comp land cost of $591,182 far exceeds the $57,379 walk-away land limit, requiring nominal city land pricing or write-down to pencil.
targets.risk: High reliance on competitive subsidy award approval and HPD disposition process.

process_note: All figures reflect preliminary screening estimates pending full site diligence, and municipal sites must be secured via competitive HPD/EDC disposition RFPs rather than standard land purchases.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
